differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Separate read and write models for peak performance.
differences.rightDesc: Unified model for reads and writes, simpler but less optimized.

differences.title: 2. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Scale read and write sides independently based on load.
differences.rightDesc: Scale both read and write operations together.

differences.title: 3. COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Requires more effort to implement and manage two models.
differences.rightDesc: Simpler to implement and understand initially.

differences.title: 4. DATA CONSISTENCY
differences.leftDesc: Read models may lag slightly behind write operations.
differences.rightDesc: Reads always reflect the latest writes.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Tailored models significantly boost read and write speeds.
differences.rightDesc: Balanced performance, can be a bottleneck for heavy loads.

differences.title: 6. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for applications with distinct read/write needs.
differences.rightDesc: Suitable for standard applications with simple data flows.

differences.title: 7. DEVELOPMENT
differences.leftDesc: Allows different teams to focus on read or write optimization.
differences.rightDesc: A single team typically manages all aspects.
